search_queries_used: site:rcem.ac.uk Mental Health in EDs Toolkit April 2023 triage assessment documentation capacity suicide
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic detention, restraint, medicine, referral or disposition
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The toolkit explicitly distinguishes current triage from prediction of future self-harm or suicide.
Legal and clinical actions remain clinician and jurisdiction decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with a primary mental-health presentation; age-specific, neurodevelopmental, intoxication, medical and safeguarding contexts require qualified review.
setting_applicability: Emergency mental-health triage, assessment and liaison working.
UAE_applicability: Assessment domains transfer, but UAE mental-health, capacity, consent, safeguarding, police, observation and transfer law and pathways govern.
recency_verification: The current RCEM April 2023 toolkit was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: April 2023 is the current official RCEM toolkit identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCEM audit standards support documenting onset or context, severity or functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, patient concerns, clinician risk context and focused mental-state assessment only when performed; they do not predict future risk.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact triage and assessment section supports recording immediate safety concerns, safeguarding, clinician-decided observation or disposition, completed discussion and arranged liaison handover only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact presenting symptoms, mental-state domains, physical causes, intoxication, capacity, current intent, access to means, protective factors, diagnosis, treatment, legal status and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
